title: coronaria flos-jovis
intro: Coronaria flos-jovis, also known as the "Flower of Jupiter," is a flowering plant in the borage family native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its seeds, which contain a compound called sanguinarine, a cardiac glycoside with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Coronaria flos-jovis include its use as a treatment for various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve heart function. Coronaria flos-jovis can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to its potential toxicity and side effects.
